programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate when naval cryptanalyst Andrew Wells is shot dead in a park outside Baltimore. At the scene, Tony spots a man who was one of his informants from 15 years earlier when he was a police officer, but who went into hiding after a bid to bring down the Russian mobsters he worked for went wrong. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team works with Abigail Borin of the Coast Guard Investigative Service after a highly suspicious explosion and fire on board an oil rig kills a US marine corps reservist. Abby detects malware in the platform's computer systems, while Ducky finds traces on the victim's body that lead to a lawyer who works for the oil company. Back in the office, a prankster is bringing some light relief and although the likeliest suspect is Tony, he denies any involvement. Crime drama, guest starring Diane Neal

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is forced to take a step back from work when his increasingly frail father Jackson begs him to help track down an old friend from the Second World War who does not have long left to live. Meanwhile, the rest of the team investigate the death of a marine sergeant who appears to have been caught in the crossfire during a bungled robbery, but Tony and Tim find evidence the victim may have been targeted. Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Petty officer Jody Ray is killed by a hit-and-run driver and evidence points to staff sergeant Justin Dunne, whose guilt seems certain when DiNozzo and McGee find him washing a bloodstained knife when they go to arrest him. However, Dunne tells his lawyer Carrie Clark he was miles away in Falls Church at the time - stabbing bookmaker Donny Sullivan to death. Unable to reveal the truth to the NCIS team, Clark decides to check out the alibi herself. Crime drama, guest starring Salli Richardson-Whitfield (A Town Called Eureka)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star in the American drama

programme.desc: The unit is called to examine the mysterious death of a sailor serving on a research vessel engaged in a top-secret mission. Drama, starring David McCallum, Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Where the Heart Is
programme.desc: Tension mounts between Karen and Jacqui, and an elderly patient confides in Chris, while David comes t o Henry's aid at Goddard's - much to Simon's displeasure. Elsewhere, a patient suspected of having cancer tries to persuade his daughter to accept the new love in his life

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe investigates a spate of garden thefts, focusing his inquiries on a horticultural landscaper whose business has blossomed since the robberies began. Unfortunately, his initial stake-out proves a disaster and, to top it all, Margaret hires the chief suspect to redesign the plot behind the restaurant. Drama, starring Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Pie in the Sky
programme.desc: Crabbe has his hands full again, with Fisher breathing down his neck over an operation to seize stolen vehicles, and a fussy food inspector causing a stir at the restaurant. Crime drama, guest starring Phyllis Logan, with Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Learning Napoleon has returned from exile, Sharpe leaves Lucille to look after their farm and returns to service on the Prince of Orange's staff. It isn't long before he's reunited with the remaining Chosen Men for one of the most momentous military engagements of all time - the Battle of Waterloo. Sean Bean, Daragh O'Malley, Hugh Fraser and Cecile Paoli star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: An alien clairvoyant dispatches Donna to a world where she never met the Doctor. Only Rose Tyler can help restore the timeline, but as the pair join forces, they hold the fate of all creation in their hands and the temp from Chiswick must face her greatest fears. Catherine Tate, David Tennant and Billie Piper star

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the Earth goes missing, the Time Lord and Donna brave the Shadow Proclamation in an attempt to discover the truth, but a terrifying old enemy waits in the background. Starring David Tennant, Catherine Tate, Billie Piper, Freema Agyeman and John Barrowman

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part two of two. The fate of the universe hangs in the balance as the Daleks activate their masterplan. With the Time Lord and Rose imprisoned, and the Tardis seemingly destroyed, the only hope of survival lies with the Doctor's secret army of companions. But as they join forces to battle Davros, Dalek Caan's prophecy declares one of them will die. Starring David Tennant, Catherine Tate and Billie Piper

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Green-fingered amateur detectives Laura and Rosemary are called in to tackle a stubborn species of weed threatening an entire year's vintage at the Crickle Valley vineyard. However, they find themselves in the right place at the right time when the body of a wine critic is discovered on the premises. Murder mystery, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The son of an ex-con under Terry's supervision is arrested for involvement in a protection racket, but is later found beaten semi-conscious by a gang of heavies. Yvonne helps a homeless man being harassed by teenagers, but when she turns a car wash power jet on one of the offenders her unprofessional conduct is caught on camera, putting her job on the line. Roger reaches rock bottom

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Max Little is pushed to the limit by his ex-wife's involvement with big-time gangster Barry Green, and Leela bonds with a busker who has been found beaten and robbed in the park, but faces a dilemma when he turns out to be an illegal immigrant. Heaton informs Yvonne her suspension will be dropped if she curbs her creative approach to policing, while Terry discovers he has a second chance with ex-wife Helen

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Rag Nymph
programme.desc: Feature-length period melodrama set in Victorian times, charting the relationship between feisty Newcastle rag-woman Aggie Winkowski and 10-year-old orphan Millie Forester. Determined to protect Millie from the seamy world of prostitution, Aggie resolves to take the youngster under her wing. Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren frets over the implementation of a new DIY table-clearing initiative, but it is the least of her worries as a lack of funds looks set to thwart her plans with Tony. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Andrew Dunn

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of the British sitcom, featuring interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show on its 20th anniversary

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a battered wife in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a women's refuge. Hetty's quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara's condition is not improving and she is kept in isolation at the hospital, leaving her friends able to do nothing but wait for more news. Dr Turner and Shelagh assist a man being held in a remand home whose wife is pregnant, while Lucille makes some new friends

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The moderator-elect of the Church of Scotland and a female companion are found naked in a car with their throats cut, but the church closes ranks as Rebus investigates the background to the case, uncovering long-buried rivalries, lies and grudges within the institution. Meanwhile, a retired police colleague in the early stages of dementia claims he is being poisoned by staff at his nursing home. Drama, starring Ken Stott, with Lorcan Cranitch, Andrew Neil and John Stahl

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: As a third victim is found murdered, Marilyn soon identifies the man responsible as Damon Morton (Iain Glen), and Walker must gather evidence to secure his conviction

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A rapist, who was sentenced to eight years by Deed, is released and immediately sets about putting a vengeful plan into action. Meanwhile, the judge presides over a case involving two policemen accused of assaulting a hunt saboteur. Drama, starring Martin Shaw

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma is forced to rearrange her plans for the festive period at the last minute, leaving Hester and William disappointed they will not get to spend their first Christmas with their grandson. However, it remains to be seen who will help them consume all the extra food and drink they have to hand. Christmas special of the comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel makes some last minute preparations for the final while Debbie gets some unwanted attention at work. Cindy is in a state of shock

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A suspect in an abduction case makes his getaway on board a bus and pulls a gun on Yvonne. The ensuing hostage situation has alarming repercussions for John Heaton, who discovers he's not the only one who's been playing away. Matt tells Emma the church they have booked has had a cancellation and they can get married in three weeks, while Terry hears wonderful news from his ex-wife

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden is preoccupied with the conjoined twins and Jayne arranges for their transfer to a hospital in London. Daisha discovers her pregnancy is beyond the legal limit for abortions and starts to consider ways to get rid of her unborn child - much to Mark's horror. After spending time with a young patient and the arrival of her fiance's sister, Faye rethinks her engagement to Joseph and their childless future

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack and Tony put their differences aside as they say their goodbyes to Eddie, while Lara and Luke are forced to acknowledge they may have been wrong about one another. Simon's bad mood makes him less than sympathetic toward staff and patients alike, and matters come to a head when Colette breaks the news to him that her child will have learning difficulties

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's the longest night of David's life when he takes Bianca on a driving lesson. And Cindy goes looking for a diversion after a night from hell with Ian

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Nigel makes some last minute preparations for the final while Debbie gets some unwanted attention at work. Cindy is in a state of shock

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Napier is intrigued by an ex-colonial couple recently returned from India who settle in Tannochbrae and refuse to have contact with their mysterious daughter. Starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew tries to convince his friend Ronnie Neale that he has a serious illness, an unenviable task at the best of times and one complicated by the prospect of losing Dawn to her estranged husband Keith. Meanwhile, confusion abounds as Sam and Joanna are called out separately to the same patient. Starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Sarah Parish

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Angus Grier's moment of triumph proves short-lived as James stumbles on his guilty secret, while Tristan faces up to telling Siegfried about his exam results, and thinks a lifestyle change might help get his brother back on side

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester thinks William has been unfaithful when he comes home from work reeking of perfume. He offers her a perfectly innocent explanation, but she continues to fear the worst. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana practise their rather inadequate midwifery skills by helping Sarah deliver her baby. Graham Crowden, Stephanie Cole and Lucy Aston star

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle enlists Rene to set up a mobile radio station to broadcast propaganda, the parts of which will be dropped by plane. Unfortunately, they fall down one of the chateau's chimneys. Second World War comedy, starring Gorden Kaye

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard finds himself with some explaining to do after Pearl discovers an incriminating long blonde hair on his person, so he turns to his friends for help. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Part one of two. The green-fingered amateur sleuths are drawn into another mystery when a barrister employing them to spruce up his garden discovers his cousin has been drowned, only for the man to be spotted alive and well a few days later. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: It seems somebody has taken the island's Day of the Dead celebrations a little too literally when a man receives a voicemail from his wife saying she is being attacked. When she turns up dead, suspicion falls upon her friends and acquaintances at the nearby yacht club - her husband Finn, club secretary Charlotte and chairman Hugh. However, Jack's curiosity is piqued by a yellow butterfly found on the victim's clothing and he calls upon Commissioner Selwyn's knowledge of such matters. Caribbean whodunit, guest starring John Sessions and Pippa Haywood

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Part one of two. Brian is suspended after assaulting a high-ranking officer in the Met, suspecting him of covering up a death in custody years before. Meanwhile, an Argentinian pistol recovered from the Thames is linked to two unsolved murders which took place 16 years apart - and with one of them having occurred in Gibraltar, Sandra takes the team - minus Brian - on an Iberian adventure to uncover the truth. Alun Armstrong, Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Denis Lawson star in the crime drama

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Danny and Fiona investigate a safe house they suspect is being used by North African terrorists. However, while planting listening devices their cover is blown and they are captured, leaving colleagues at MI5 with a difficult dilemma. The terrorists are demanding nothing less than the complete withdrawal of British forces from Iraq, or they will kill the agents. Adam's concern for his wife clouds his judgement and he agrees to negotiate, not realising he is walking into a trap

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey's partying catches up with him when he wakes up in a strange bed and ends up being grilled at Mill Street station on rape charges. Will takes a blood sample from an unconscious driver who has knocked a courier off his bike, and takes advice from Matt about whether it is admissible evidence in court. When the sample goes missing, he jumps to conclusions about his love-rival's determination to make life difficult for him

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: Confederate belle Emma is overwhelmed by her friend Tom's mental state. Dr Foster wrestles with his marriage and career as Mary tries to improve her patients' lives with Samuel's help. Aurelia submits to Silas's advances in exchange for his help

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry VIII is desperate to end his marriage to Katherine of Aragon, but Cardinal Wolsey's efforts to persuade the pope to grant an annulment are fruitless - much to the dismay of Thomas Cromwell, a lawyer dependent on Wolsey's patronage. The king's new mistress is rumoured to be Anne Boleyn, who has sworn vengeance on Wolsey after he refused to let her marry Harry Percy, so Cromwell visits her in the hope of swaying her to the cardinal's side. Historical drama adapted by Peter Straughan from Hilary Mantel's Booker Prize-winning novels Wolf Hall and Bring Up the Bodies, and starring Mark Rylance, Damian Lewis, Claire Foy and Jonathan Pryce

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A Shropshire couple sample life in Melbourne for a week, but while Rob is keen to make a fresh start, Selina is having doubts about moving away from friends and family

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits the Clink restaurant in Styal near Manchester, where prisoners are being given the chance of a fresh start by being trained in a fine-dining restaurant

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple find a characterful country house in the North Wessex Downs on a budget of £775,000, and also pays a visit to Highclere Castle in Wiltshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Katie Keat attempts to bring a wooden swivel seat back to its best, while Sarah has a collection of cutlery to find a use for

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie Edwards, Jesy Nelson, Leigh-Anne Pinnock and Jade Thirlwall continue their talent search, this time focusing on finding a rap R&B group. The acts are auditioned individually, then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls before the final one is chosen

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: When he and Albie come face to face in Barcelona, Douglas tries to make amends for his actions and his son prepares to give his dad another chance - but disaster soon strikes. Will a near-death experience, the unearthing of secrets, and Douglas's dogged determination to keep his family together be enough to change Connie's feelings?

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The performance artist invites Alan Yentob into her home, opens up her enormous personal archive, and travels back to her birthplace of Belgrade. Abramovic talks about her family and growing up in communist Yugoslavia, where she fell in love with performance art and became one of its most outrageous and celebrated practitioners, and discusses turning her life into art

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: The lives of the squad are turned upside down when the Women's Super League is shut down due to the pandemic midway through the season

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before chatting to guests, both in the studio and remotely. Strictl head judge Shirley Ballas discusses her autobiography Behind the Sequins, Hollywood star Ewan McGregor discusses being back on the road for his latest travelogue Long Way Up, dancer and presenter Ashley Banjo promotes the new Diversity tour Connected, while music superstar Miley Cyrus chats and performs her current single Midnight Sky. Plus, members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Angela Rippon reveal how banks are leaving some victims of fraud high and dry, despite new protections having been introduced

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell hear from the new owners of a house in Erdington, Birmingham, and six holiday homes in an old vicarage in Llanfynydd, Carmarthenshire

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme exploring what it takes to police Britain's roads, including footage of a Metropolitan Police operation to tackle moped-enabled crime

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An Aberdeen crew on nightshift searches for a woman at risk of hypothermia, while the air ambulance transfers a patient from his home on Lewis to Glasgow for hospital treatment

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion is in Leominster, Herefordshire, where experts Tim Weeks and Kate Bliss help two teams search for antiques that they hope will make them a profit at auction

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The return of Basil and Robert Thomas - the sons of a local mine owner - causes Prem and his wife Kamini to face the prospect of becoming outcasts once again, while others welcome the duo with open arms. Drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's knack for gardening as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo also look at the country's passion for growing vegetables at home, a skill that has inspired some amazing dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in Oxfordshire on a budget of £900,000, and finds out about charcoal-making the environmentally friendly way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and David and Harry Rich transform a garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex, into the perfect place for John and David to host their wedding reception

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of wine, and a retro tin toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine a play date with a polar bear, a dolphin that thinks it is a sperm whale, and the ducklings with a cat for a mum

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Featuring Norfolk's World Championship Thumb Wrestling

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: CBBC star Sam Nixon invites viewers into his back garden to make pizza, BGT semi-finalist Yakub performs a dance routine live in the studio and brothers Cam, Tyler and Aaron reveal what it's like to live and work in a zoo as part of their CBBC series One Zoo Three

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia moves into a new apartment with her best friends, Vinny and Poppy

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia encounters a passive-aggressive lift

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is on a mission to find the weirdest dinosaur ever

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox reveals how the unique properties of light provide an insight into the origins and evolution of both mankind and the universe. He charts the importance of light in the development of the world's ancient civilisations, and explains how the speed of light allows the measurement of both distance and time - before pinpointing one of the most important moments in the evolution of life on Earth

programme.title: Monkey Kingdom (2015)
programme.desc: Documentary following a newborn monkey and its mother as they struggle to survive within the competitive social hierarchy of the Temple Troop, a group of simians who live in ancient ruins found deep in the jungles of Sri Lanka. Narrated by Tina Fey

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: As winter morphs into spring, lambing and calving keeps the team busy out on the farms. However, their small animal clinic is also packed with pets and Martin Fitzpatrick - the man who founded the practice 35 years ago - faces a difficult case concerning a Highland terrier. Young vet Darren is called out on an emergency to a farm where a prize cow is struggling to deliver a calf. An examination reveals it's simply too big to come out naturally. Darren decides the safest option, for both the cow and the calf, is to perform an emergency Caesarean section. It's a risky operation, but without it, the cow will die

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Newly qualified vet Patrick is called out to help a farmer whose cow has been struggling all night with a difficult labour. Patrick discovers her calf is breached, and has to reposition it. However, once it's in the correct position, a shock discovery makes his job twice as precarious. Back in Castlewellan, a farmer arrives with a two-week-old lamb that's developed a limp. Some antibiotics and a dose of anti-inflammatories will help the lamb back on its feet. However, vet Maeve notices its eyes are milky white and she has to use all her expertise and knowledge to save the lamb's sight

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Japan by heading into the heart of the country to find out about the historic roots of Japanese cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly Come Dancing footwork into action when kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo take an awe-inspiring bike ride to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by a geisha in Kyoto, complete with drinking games

programme.title: Queen of Katwe (2016)
programme.desc: The life of a poor Ugandan youngster is changed for ever when she is introduced to the game of chess. Drama, starring David Oyelowo, Lupita Nyong'o and Madina Nalwanga

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on animals' relationships with each other and with humans. Hugh discovers how the capuchin monkeys of Costa Rica have such a fantastic team spirit, biologist Patrick Aryee investigates man's relationship with the vulture - nature's most infamous scavenger - and zoologist Lucy Cooke finds out about the medicine in Kentucky that may improve a rare pig's relationship problems. Conservationist Giles Clark ventures into the rainforests of Thailand to find out how a clever matchmaking technique could help save the clouded leopard from extinction

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Natasha Raskin search for antiques at Watts Gallery in Surrey, and Paul Martin uncovers the story of the ceramicist and designer Mary Watts

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces the key third-round action from the European Tour's traditional flagship event at Wentworth's famous West Course. Reigning champion Danny Willett will be among the British and global stars hoping to still be in contention at this stage of the rescheduled tournament, which is being held without fans because of the coronavirus pandemic. Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter provide the commentary

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford explores how planners and architects took to Scotland's skies after the Second World War as they embarked on missions to redesign towns and cities. From building a motorway right through the heart of Glasgow, to one of Scotland's first tower blocks, James reveals how these utopian and controversial visions have transformed lives of people living in Scotland today

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: A look across Hawaii's varied islands, discovering how they were made, and how its wildlife thrives. The most remote island chain on Earth, its tropical shores are hard to reach. However, for the hardy creatures that have made it there, such as the waterfall-climbing fish, carnivorous caterpillar and Laysan albatross, a land of opportunity awaits. From newly formed lava fields, to lush jungles, and vibrant coral reefs, these diverse and beautiful islands have it all. As this documentary reveals, its people are looking to the future and attempting to protect and foster its flora and fauna

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes the quiz in a 'ridiculous' direction this evening. Trying to come up with the answers are Alan Davies, Maisie Adam, David Mitchell and Holly Walsh

programme.title: Teddy Pendergrass: If You Don't Know Me
programme.desc: Documentary about the life, music and towering achievements of soul singer Teddy Pendergrass, from his origins and early life in Philadelphia. The film charts his early successes with Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes and his solo superstardom and sex symbol status, before moving on to his later life and continued success after overcoming the car crash that left him paralysed

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: Slavery once drove the economy of the American South and when it was finally abolished in 1863, its gospel tradition lived on. As the black American church grew in strength and popularity, so did its singers. A network of performers emerged known as the gospel highway and in the 1950s, soul music's first generation of stars emerged. The genre delivered an assertive, integrated vision of black America during the Civil Rights era of the early 1960s, presided over by the likes of Otis Redding and Aretha Franklin. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Ghost Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Professor Philip Goodman, famous for debunking hoaxes and ghost sightings, is given a dossier of three terrifying, unsolvable cases by his hero and role model Charles Cameron, who has been missing, presumed dead, for years. Can Goodman explain what even Cameron could not? Horror anthology, starring Andy Nyman, Martin Freeman and Paul Whitehouse

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter J, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As autumn gets underway, Monty Don gives advice on what plants need to be taken under cover now, what can stay out until after the first frosts and others that just need an extra layer of protection. Plus, he shows what can be planted now in the vegetable garden. Joe Swift visits Good Morning Britain presenter Kate Garraway in her London garden and discusses how it has been a sanctuary over the months her husband has been seriously ill. The programme also returns to Nick Bailey's home to see the results of the design project he started in his own back garden earlier in the year and meets a Kent woman who has filled her garden with exotic and tropical plants

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond walks in the footsteps of the old cattle drovers and finds out about the annual cattle sale in Crieff, the centre of the Scottish cattle trade. Anne Lundon investigates how the huge influx of campervans this year is impacting Scotland's roads and Euan McIlwraith explores how beef labelling will be affected by leaving the EU, and Arlene Stuart meets a couple who distil their own rum

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson celebrates the humble apple pie on his allotment and Carole Baxter revisits a hedgerow she helped plant 17 years ago to see it in all its berry bird-feeding glory. Kirsty Wilson creates a 'desertscape' with cacti in her Edinburgh flat and there's a tour of Branklyn Gardens, Perth, with head gardener Alistair Chalmers

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary heads to the Yorkshire coast to celebrate a unique bonfire party, explore the ancient seaport of Whitby and sample some classic, coastal comfort food - fish and chips and ice-cream. Mary visits a small smokehouse that has been run by the same family for five generations, where she helps smoke herring to make their world-renowned kippers. To end her time in Yorkshire, Mary helps a local community with their unique bonfire for Guy Fawkes Night. Inspired by all she sees, Mary makes a rustic smoked trout pate, followed by a one-pot haddock and shrimp feast

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of baking recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes Jasper Carrott back to Barry Island in south Wales, where the comedian reminisces about a childhood holiday he had there in 1958

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the careers of some of the most successful directors in movie history, including John Ford, Frank Capra, Sidney Lumet, George Cukor, Billy Wilder and Cecil B DeMille, among others. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Rio Bravo (1959)
programme.desc: Sheriff John T Chance and his drunken deputy Dude make a courageous stand against a rancher and his hired guns, who are threatening to tear apart a peace-loving town if a murderer being held in the local jail is not released. Howard Hawks' Western, starring John Wayne, Dean Martin, Ricky Nelson, Walter Brennan and Angie Dickinson, who won a Golden Globe for Most Promising Newcomer for her portrayal of Feathers

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents an edition from Bristol Cathedral. Natasha Raskin Sharp notices a painting of an apothecary on a piece of tin, and James Lewis looks at a valuable royal ring

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Irish resort of Skerries, the crew run to the station when strong winds sweep a teenager on a paddleboard towards the busy shipping lanes of the Irish Sea, while in the Bristol Channel, among the many yachts taking advantage of a fresh summer breeze, one loses a crew member overboard. While the crowds are heading home at the end of a busy summer's day in Cleethorpes, two 13-year-old girls find themselves alone, cut off and at risk of drowning on a sandbank rapidly disappearing under a fast-rising tide

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces the final day's play from the European Tour's traditional flagship event at Wentworth's famous West Course. Last year, Englishman Danny Willett earned arguably his biggest win since the 2016 US Masters when he birdied the last two holes to seal a three-stroke triumph over world number six Jon Rahm. Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter provide the commentary

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones consider three more money-making proposals. The ideas under deliberation are an environmentally friendly range of plastic-free water bottles made from starch, ergonomic underwear, and an armchair tray for elderly people. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin looks back on the making of Pole to Pole, in which he travelled along a single line of longitude across the USSR, Africa and finally into Antarctica. Along the way, he paid a visit to Chernobyl and gained an insight into the last days of the Soviet Union. His travels across Africa led to an encounter with some fellow globetrotters from his home town of Sheffield - but he was forced to make a detour when his planned route passed through a war zone

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at 400 years of human trafficking from Africa to the New World, from the perspective of three different storylines. In the first episode, the actor travels to Gabon on the west coast of central Africa, where captives were herded onto ships

programme.title: Enslaved with Samuel L Jackson
programme.desc: A look at 400 years of human trafficking from Africa to the New World, from the perspective of three different storylines. The team continue their detailed examination of how 12 million Africans were shipped to the Americas

programme.title: A United Kingdom (2016)
programme.desc: King Seretse Khama of Botswana put his kingdom into political and diplomatic turmoil after falling for Ruth Williams, a London office worker. Fact-based drama from Belle director Amma Assante, starring David Oyelowo and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Following their trip into the future, Hiro and Ando become even more determined to save the world, and Nathan makes plans to win the election. Sylar decides to visit his mother, while Thompson introduces a new person into the organisation

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Hiro receives information regarding his family as he prepares to face Sylar to the death. Peter and Ted make plans to leave New York in an effort to avoid the explosion, while Nathan's mother offers him advice about Linderman. Starring Eric Roberts and George Takei

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The presenter ventures out from her home study, and her first stop is Stonehenge, a British cultural icon she has revered as a child. She has rare access to the stone circle where she meets leading cultural voices, including Turner Prize-winning artist Jeremy Deller and one of Britain's best-known actresses, Vanessa Redgrave. Mary also explores which cultural forms are the winners and losers of the pandemic

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A spate of burglaries at Christmas leads to a police community support officer and his colleague being attacked, as they track down and try to arrest the thief. A West Midlands police officer returns to work after an assault, only to be attacked again just weeks later. Plus, a West Yorkshire police officer starts his retirement needing physiotherapy after being attacked

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks back at some of the most eclectic toys and games bought by the teams over the years, and offers advice on how to spot a bargain. Plus, Charlie Ross learns about an early board games manufacturer from Gloucester

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson takes a look at one of the oldest and rarest plants known to man, the Wollemi pine, sometimes called the fossil tree. George Anderson shows how to naturalise new bulbs in grass, and Carole Baxter, using techniques borrowed from her father, reviews her tomatoes and sees if her dad has it right

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. However, if they are Decimated, they will leave with nothing. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Golf: PGA Championship
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour introduces a round-up of action from the final two days of the European Tour's traditional flagship event at Wentworth's famous West Course. Peter Alliss, Ken Brown and Andrew Cotter provide the commentary

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Martyn Colbeck and Cynthia Moss examine the legacy of Echo the elephant, who died in 2009 after being followed on film during the final chapter of her life, having guided her family for half a century. With a drought devastating the animals' home in the shadow of Kilimanjaro, a 38-strong band of relatives and descendants must overcome the loss of their leader, hunger and the threat of poachers to survive. Presented by David Attenborough

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott celebrates dishes from across the Channel, getting inspiration from France, Italy and Spain. The menu includes the Greedy Italians' linguine, James Martin's Spanish sardines, a chicken dish from Raymond Blanc, and a Bake Off masterclass on how to make a classic Greek spanakopita

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside. David Fletcher discovers scripts from the TV series Lovejoy, and Jonathan Pratt spots Beatles dolls

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: The quiz returns, with Ade Adepitan, Jean Johansson, Stephen Mangan and Vikki Stone going toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Skipper Paul notices that his boat is on a collision course with another trawler off the Scottish coast, and takes evasive action to ensure that the boats and nets avoid each other. Meanwhile, in the remote Norwegian sector of the North Sea, skipper Jonny McLeman goes for one last haul of fish and then starts the 24-hour journey back to Peterhead

programme.title: Rob Burrow: My Year with MND
programme.desc: Documentary following the former England and Leeds Rhino rugby league player as he deals with motor neurone disease, with contributions from family and friends. As the disease takes its toll physically, Burrow speaks of his determination to live as normal a life as possible, and explains how his rugby career has prepared him for the challenge

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer question on the specialist subjects of Manchester United, the novels of Toni Morrison, Berthe Morisot, and William Tyndale, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as the Puzzle Hunters take on the Walruses. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Mile High Club, Subway Footlongs, Edinburgh's Royal Mile, and A Miner's Inch

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The last first-round match sees four students from Magdalene College, Cambridge take on a team from University College, Oxford for a place in the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Nearly five years on from Jim Foley's execution at the hands of Jihadi John, Diane Foley is fighting for justice, while the Muellers continue their search for answers about their missing daughter Kayla. In London a court case in the UK Supreme Court threatens Diane's campaign to see the suspects face a trial in the US, while the Muellers are conducting their own investigation, supported by contacts working secretly in Syria

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: James Acaster, Tom Allen, Ed Byrne, Rhys James and Ellie Taylor join host Dara O Briain and regular participant Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at the week's events through the medium of fast-thinking and improvisation

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Being Blacker (2018)
programme.desc: Molly Dineen's documentary about Blacker Dread, a Jamaican-born reggae producer, businessman, father, son and pillar of the community in Brixton. The film focuses on a tumultuous time in his life as he deals with the death of his mother and the prospect of his first prison sentence

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A 999 call for a baby having a fit is a priority call, and crewmates Kate and Terry are immediately dispatched. They are 2.5 miles from the Category One emergency, but the traffic jams lead to a fraught journey for the crew. Trainee call handler Mackenzie, who is on her final shift of training, is put to the test when she takes a call from a woman in late-stage labour with twins

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: The return of the documentary using bodycam and CCTV footage to tell the stories of emergency service workers assaulted in the line of duty. Police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight when a moped chase ends in chaos

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer sets off from Little Diomede Island in the Bering Strait on an epic 50,000-mile trek around the Pacific Rim. On the first stage of his journey across Russia, he travels through the notorious Kolyma region via the hot springs of Kamchatka. He then makes a guest appearance with the Russian Pacific Fleet Song and Dance Ensemble to sing one of Stalin's favourite patriotic anthems

programme.title: Inspector Montalbano
programme.desc: It is high summer in Vigata and the detective investigates when the body of Agata Cosentino is found in the municipal archives where she worked. But the building was supposed to be closed for the holidays, so what was the young woman doing there? Italian crime drama, starring Luca Zingaretti

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: A prominent Danish gender campaigner is found dead in Malmo, meaning that Swedish detective Saga Noren must once again collaborate with a counterpart in the Danish police as she investigates. With Martin Rohde now in gaol for murder, Saga is assigned a new colleague in Copenhagen, but the pair's working relationship fails to get off to a good start, a fact only exacerbated by Saga's role in getting well-liked Danish cop Martin sent down. Starring Sofia Helin and Kirsten Olesen. In Swedish and Danish

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Malmo detective Saga and her Copenhagen colleague Hanne must put aside their differences to catch the perpetrator of the so-called 'Clown Murders'. A prime suspect arrives in the form of the first victim's apparently traumatised son, who has gone on the run. Meanwhile, Saga is distracted from her investigation by the untimely arrival of her mother, who insists on showing her a medical file that she hopes will put an end to their estrangement. Crime drama, in Swedish and Danish, starring Sofia Helin and Kirsten Olesen

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the edition first aired on January 4, featuring performances by the Quireboys, Sonia and Latino Rave

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer sets off from Little Diomede Island in the Bering Strait on an epic 50,000-mile trek around the Pacific Rim. programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population

programme.title: Mud, Sweat and Tractors: The Story of Agriculture
programme.desc: Exploring man's relationship with the land, charting the revolution that food production in Britain underwent in the 20th century and examining the impact this had on the lives of farmers and consumers alike. The first programme features the home movies of two dairy farmers from the south of England and looks at the changing ways in which milk was distributed and sold

programme.title: Yellowstone
programme.desc: Following the wildlife that inhabits the famous American national park, a lost world of deep forests, vast plains and bubbling geysers. The first episode focuses on a variety of species as they struggle to survive the rigours of a freezing six-month winter, revealing that although the herds of elk and bison are weakened by the cold, one animal gets stronger - the wolf

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: After 13,000 miles, Michael Palin's setback in Cape Town forces a galling compromise in his itinerary, and there are few options still open if he is to reach the southernmost extremity of Earth. The odds seem stacked against the successful completion of the intrepid funnyman's epic journey

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The new techniques being pioneered by vision-impaired astronomers at Jodrell Bank to see the universe, using their senses of hearing and touch

programme.title: The Guardians (2017)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter take over the work on a farm when their men leave for the front in 1915 Paris. When Hortense hires Francine, a young ward of the estate to help them, Francine believes she has finally found a family. In French

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould return for one final commission, helping Under-Sheriff and Secondary of London Charles Henty investigate whether two paintings in his possession are as valuable as he thinks. The first is a landscape picture of Dedham in Essex, thought to have been painted by pre-eminent British artist Alfred Munnings, and the second painting is of a medieval French village, and was rumoured to have been produced by part-time painter, full-time prime minister Winston Churchill. Charles hopes the sale of these two potentially pricey heirlooms will help protect jobs at a working French farm he inherited from his late uncle, but first he must learn whether the pictures are truly genuine

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: In this programme we visit three children who are all having a fascinating day out. In Russia, seven-year-old Liza picks blueberries with her grandma - and helps turn them into delicious pancakes. In Ghana, Tyra, who's five, goes to the market with her mum and learns how to do the shopping. And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. We see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and discoveries of childhood are universal.

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of ordinary children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having a very busy day. In Jordan, seven-year-old Hamza is having trouble getting up for school. But will he get there in time? And after school in India, six-year-old Anushka learns how to draw patterns on the ground called Rangoli with her aunt. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.
