programme.desc: Twins Daisy and Lily take a trip to the beach with their cousins. Dogs Foxy and Saffy join in too, and love chasing after balls on the sand. After all the seaside fun, the whole family heads to the beach cafe to enjoy an ice-cream. Back at home, the sisters take care of a special visitor - Grandma Owl the school cuddly toy. Together they embark on an adventure around the house and take lots of photos

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: It's bedtime and Maddie is finding out about different things we sleep on and in. At a campsite she uses a special thermal camera to show how the layers in a sleeping bag work by trapping warm air inside to keep us warm

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain uses a song to teach Cook and Line how to speak like pirates, while four young swashbucklers try to help Gem reclaim her jewels

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It's winter and it's dark and too cold to play outside. The squirrels wonder what Duggee is up to - he seems to be looking through a long stick out of the window. Duggee explains that it's a telescope, and he's looking at things far away in space. The squirrels are keen to have a go too, but first Duggee tells them about the Solar System, and in order to help him they must all dress up as planets

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter makes a rash promise to appease an angry Tommy Brock which leaves him and his friends worming for their lives

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Delivery Duckling arrives and tells Love Monster he has been chosen to be sipping host at a special Hot Chocolate Day ceremony. She then cycles off before telling him what a sipping host does. Love Monster goes to Elder Panda to ask his advice, and he tells him there are many ways to serve hot chocolate - each person needs to find their own unique way. Elder Panda adds honey and banana from his garden to his. Love Monster starts the ceremony by adding his signature marshmallows, but then Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny announces she puts marshmallows in hers too. Love Monster thinks he needs to use something more unusual, so he adds pickles, but this doesn’t go down well - in fact, it tastes horrible! It turns out the reason why he was chosen was so that he could make hot chocolate the way he always makes it. He does, and everyone loves it.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move in a really cool way! Grace has a drive in a lowrider and makes it bounce, shows off a car that can drive over other cars in a traffic jam, and does some cool donuts in a drift car.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: The presenter travels back in time to the era of the Triceratops, a plant-eating dinosaur which roamed the plains of North America

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz has to unfreeze Bob, Bevel and Purl and get a tiara back from two castle trolls

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are relaxing by the Dead Sea when Grandmaster Glitch causes a custard catastrophe - to the Vroomster

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: James has organised a bike class, but Caitlin is too proud to admit she can't ride a bike. Euan tries to persuade her to own up, but she won't listen. Can Molly save the day?

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Fun for younger viewers featuring tales of the world's worst puppy who woofs and wags his way into the lives of an unassuming family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran put on a puppet show in the garden using odd socks!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The duo take the Shallots to see the Ribbon Sisters perform with their family circus

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: After Colly Wobble loses her shows, Moon Baby shows her a magical shoe tree in Storyland

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines are left behind after everyone goes for a ride on the Ninky Nonk, but when they eventually catch it, they find a special carriage to ride in

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway climbs the Nash Point lighthouse, a place he used to visit as a child. His coastal walk takes him along the 'Jurassic' Glamorgan coastline. On the way he goes fossil hunting, searches for an elusive chough and learns the story of a pregnancy-testing frog. He begins his walk at St Donat's Castle, once owned by newspaper magnate Sir Randolph Hearst, which became a party palace for the rich and famous, then heads for Dunraven Bay

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A Shropshire couple sample life in Melbourne for a week, but while Rob is keen to make a fresh start, Selina is having doubts about moving away from friends and family

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Claire McCollum visits the Clink restaurant in Styal near Manchester, where prisoners are being given the chance of a fresh start by being trained in a fine-dining restaurant

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple find a characterful country house in the North Wessex Downs on a budget of £775,000, and also pays a visit to Highclere Castle in Wiltshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Walsall Recycling Centre, where he saves a set of lathe tools, a sofa and a collection of plant pots in the hope of breathing new life into them

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Revamping their Norwich front garden has left Jane and Terry less than happy with their existing back garden, so the pair enlist Charlie Dimmock and the Harry and David Rich brothers to come up with a plan for a contemporary cottage garden. However, the budget they set for realising their dreams proves exacting to meet

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway is in fine voice when he kicks off his walk in Cardigan, home of the first Eisteddfod. After visiting the castle, lovingly restored by locals, he learns about the dying 'art' of Seine net fishing before climbing the famous mount at Mwnt. He also gets to feed an alpaca and take tea with the Women's Institute at his final destination, Aberporth - where he meets a fellow meteorologist, 98-year-old Joan

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer gains exclusive access to explore the usually inaccessible locations that are home to some of the biggest engineering projects in Wales, both past and present. He kayaks over the highest navigable aqueduct in the world, visits the vital repair work to an underground Victorian engineering marvel, witnesses the defueling of Wales' last nuclear reactor and discovers how one of the biggest freight planes the world has ever seen is constructed

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: Life becomes harder for the mining men of Coal House when they attempt to master new skills, and their wages becomes linked to their performance. When the wives and children back home enjoy Halloween celebrations, the families realise their extravagance may have left them verging on bankruptcy

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie Edwards, Jesy Nelson, Leigh-Anne Pinnock and Jade Thirlwall continue their talent search, this time focusing on finding a rap R&B group. The acts are auditioned individually, then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls before the final one is chosen

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness each buy a sporty insurance write-off for £6,000 and take part in three challenges at the programme's test track before they're ordered to make their cars road legal and report to Alexandra Palace, where the producers have constructed a monstrous Wall of Death. The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: When he and Albie come face to face in Barcelona, Douglas tries to make amends for his actions and his son prepares to give his dad another chance - but disaster soon strikes. Will a near-death experience, the unearthing of secrets, and Douglas's dogged determination to keep his family together be enough to change Connie's feelings?

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Republic of Ireland v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents action from the UEFA Nations League Group B4 match, which took place at Aviva Stadium in Dublin, where Wales were looking to maintain their 100 per cent record in the group. With analysis from Neil Taylor

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The performance artist invites Alan Yentob into her home, opens up her enormous personal archive, and travels back to her birthplace of Belgrade. Abramovic talks about her family and growing up in communist Yugoslavia, where she fell in love with performance art and became one of its most outrageous and celebrated practitioners, and discusses turning her life into art

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, and a look at the biggest issues in the women's game. Arsenal are the defending champions, but face stiff competition from the likes of Chelsea, Manchester City and Manchester United

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: The lives of the squad are turned upside down when the Women's Super League is shut down due to the pandemic midway through the season

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host reviews trending news stories before chatting to guests, both in the studio and remotely. Strictl head judge Shirley Ballas discusses her autobiography Behind the Sequins, Hollywood star Ewan McGregor discusses being back on the road for his latest travelogue Long Way Up, dancer and presenter Ashley Banjo promotes the new Diversity tour Connected, while music superstar Miley Cyrus chats and performs her current single Midnight Sky. Plus, members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Angela Rippon reveal how banks are leaving some victims of fraud high and dry, despite new protections having been introduced

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell hear from the new owners of a house in Erdington, Birmingham, and six holiday homes in an old vicarage in Llanfynydd, Carmarthenshire

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A special edition of the programme exploring what it takes to police Britain's roads, including footage of a Metropolitan Police operation to tackle moped-enabled crime

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: An Aberdeen crew on nightshift searches for a woman at risk of hypothermia, while the air ambulance transfers a patient from his home on Lewis to Glasgow for hospital treatment

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: The return of Basil and Robert Thomas - the sons of a local mine owner - causes Prem and his wife Kamini to face the prospect of becoming outcasts once again, while others welcome the duo with open arms. Drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore Britain's knack for gardening as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo also look at the country's passion for growing vegetables at home, a skill that has inspired some amazing dishes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a property in Oxfordshire on a budget of £900,000, and finds out about charcoal-making the environmentally friendly way

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and David and Harry Rich transform a garden in Shoreham-by-Sea, West Sussex, into the perfect place for John and David to host their wedding reception

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against each other to buy four new items - a vintage Steiff teddy bear, a two-headed Georgian nutcracker, a very special bottle of wine, and a retro tin toy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Mo Jannah tries out e-scooters as he finds out about plans to bring them to Welsh cities and the programme hears from a family left divided by the building work on their new home. Plus, Steffan Powell gets a dummy's guide to wearing a face mask

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Katie Keat attempts to bring a wooden swivel seat back to its best, while Sarah has a collection of cutlery to find a use for

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Perrie Edwards, Jesy Nelson, Leigh-Anne Pinnock and Jade Thirlwall continue their talent search, this time focusing on finding a rap R&B group. The acts are auditioned individually, then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls before the final one is chosen

programme.title: Top Gear

The trio also celebrate the era of the 200mph supercar by going on a road trip in a Ferrari F40, a Lamborghini Diablo, and a Jaguar XJ220

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: When he and Albie come face to face in Barcelona, Douglas tries to make amends for his actions and his son prepares to give his dad another chance - but disaster soon strikes. Will a near-death experience, the unearthing of secrets, and Douglas's dogged determination to keep his family together be enough to change Connie's feelings?

programme.title: Sportscene

programme.desc: The performance artist invites Alan Yentob into her home, opens up her enormous personal archive, and travels back to her birthplace of Belgrade. programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Scottish National Party

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The death of transport worker Belly Mujinga following reports she had been coughed and spat on by a customer at London's Victoria Station was described by Boris Johnson as appalling and has prompted two million people to join a Justice for Belly campaign. A police investigation wasn't launched until five weeks after the mother-of-one had died from Covid-19 and concluded there was insufficient evidence to charge anyone with a crime. Reporter Rianna Croxford goes back over the evidence and asks whether all available lines of inquiry were followed, and examines claims that Belly could have been better protected at work and hears calls for an inquest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda worries that Mick is being distant so goes to find Tina, who reassures her that his behaviour has nothing to do with her. Ben shares his concerns with Callum about Phil seeing Raymond, and what will happen if Denise finds out - but unknown to them, Lexi overhears their conversation. Gray encourages Whitney to tell the truth in her final statements, while Jean and Kat are shocked by a photo of Ruby and Martin on social media, and try to keep the news from Stacey

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike makes a discovery among some old relics in the garden, which leads Thomas to go down memory lane with Alison and share a story from his past that he has never told before. Meanwhile, Lady Button, Pat and the Captain have gathered to hear Julian give one of his scheduled talks, but curiosity sends them upstairs and they can't resist getting involved with Thomas's tale as well

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: The genealogy documentary follows more celebrities as they trace their family trees to uncover facts about their heritage. First up is Doctor Who star Jodie Whittaker, who looks into a romantic story she has been told about how her paternal grandmother Greta came to be given the middle name of Verdun - which was a First World War battle. On her mother's side, Jodie gets to the bottom of how her great-great-grandfather worked his way up from child labourer in a Yorkshire coal mine to becoming the owner of one

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Darren Harriott hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Birmingham, recalling the colourful characters he met while working the door at Digbeth's hottest night spots. He then introduces three guests, with Bella Hull making her TV debut with some excellent time saving tips, Texas-born Kemah Bob discussing the differences between Americans and Brits, and Chris Washington filling the audience in on lockdown fitness pursuits

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob follows the comedian and actor as he publishes his autobiography. Lenny meets up with his closest friends, family and colleagues to remember his sudden rise to fame at the age of 16 on TV talent show New Faces, which catapulted him from working-class West Midlands kid to one of Britain's most celebrated black performers. Alan explores his other TV roles on Tiswas and Three of a Kind, as well as his controversial time as the only black performer on The Black and White Minstrel Show. Lenny also discusses his recent career reinvention as a serious actor and his work as a political activist campaigning for greater diversity in entertainment and broadcasting

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Conservative MP Nicky Morgan joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Jason Atherton and special guest Vic Reeves. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain focuses on something close to her heart - baking delicious treats to share with loved ones. Nadiya comes from a large family, so baking for others has always taken centre stage in her own home. She kicks things off with her chilli and cranberry brioche wreath, featuring fluffy indulgent dough balls surrounding an entire baked camembert cheese, creating the ultimate tear and share. Over the years, Nadiya has become an expert in catering for big family gatherings, and she has learnt that there is nothing better for impressing guests than a gigantic stack of delicious desserts. Her mini pecan pies filled with clotted cream ensure that everyone has their own little parcel of perfection. Next up is a cheat's bread-and-butter pudding, using ice-cream and shop-bought croissants to slash the traditional cooking time without compromising on flavour

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's Nations League fixtures, including England v Belgium and Republic of Ireland v Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts catches up with a development he first saw in Anthorn, Cumbria in 2012, and the team follows projects in Hounslow, west London, and Crewe in Cheshire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help competing teams bag the best buys to make a profit at auction

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps couple looking for a home in Ceredigion on a budget of £355,000, and also heads to the small town of Llandysul to meet a dynamic dairy farming family

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms three items from the Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a collection of fishing rods, and a reproduction designer chair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brother's face one of their hardest ever garden rescues to date in Stowmarket, turning a concrete space into a family friendly back garden

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants Marcus Brigstocke, Helen Brigstocke, David Seaman, Frankie Seaman, Naughty Boy, Aisha Khan, Toby Tarrant and Fia Tarrant take part

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Shirley and Siobhan from London, radio DJs and best friends Mike and Brian from Glasgow, and engaged couple Rachel and Raj from Hampshire

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: Little Mix band members Perrie, Jesy, Leigh-Anne and Jade search for a female dance group, with the all-singing, all-dancing hopefuls auditioning individually before being tested alongside each other, with only best making the final line up and going through to the performance shows

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: In the final episode, Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, the judges and the show's professional dancers look back at some of the most memorable moments from Strictly Come Dancing finals, reminiscing about some of the most emotional and nerve-wracking nights inside the Strictly ballroom. This special episode also pays tribute to the 2014 Strictly champion, Caroline Flack

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the second episode, Jack and Michaela, a young couple from Wigan, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014)
programme.desc: The superhuman soldier becomes an operative for secret intelligence agency SHIELD, but grows suspicious of their motives. Following a battle with a mysterious cyborg assassin, he and fellow Avenger Black Widow become fugitives, and try to bring down a sinister conspiracy. Comic-book thriller sequel, starring Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Samuel L Jackson and Robert Redford

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Chicago Bears and Tampa Bay Buccaneers. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world, as the leading sides in the NFL continue their bids to succeed Kansas City Chiefs as Super Bowl-winners

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: A Shropshire couple sample life in Melbourne for a week, but while Rob is keen to make a fresh start, Selina is having doubts about moving away from friends and family

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp is joined by experts Danny Sebastian and David Harper at Nottingham racecourse as two teams are challenged to scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell for profit at auction. Natasha also goes behind the scenes at the National Justice Museum, which is based in Nottingham

programme.title: Songs of Praise

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey. Katie Keat attempts to bring a wooden swivel seat back to its best, while Sarah has a collection of cutlery to find a use for

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are tasked to create a garden that fuses Japan and Yorkshire - in Cheshunt, Hertfordshire. Yorkshire-born James wants to see hints of his native county in his new Home Counties house, while his Japanese wife Akika, who he met when traveling, naturally wants to feel a link to her upbringing and homeland

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In an edition focusing on the harvest, Adam Henson takes viewers through the toughest one of his career on his Gloucestershire farm. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith picks grapes from sun-ripened Welsh vines and Matt Baker races to keep up with the booming strawberry harvest in Yorkshire

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In a special edition of the programme, HARDtalk is in northwest England, to the area known as the Lake District. The landscape is beautiful but is not wild. The fields have been shaped by generations of shepherds and stockmen. Stephen Sackur speaks to James Rebanks whose farm has been in his family's hands for at least 600 years. He has become a nationally renowned advocate for a better kind of farming that is more sustainable and environmentally responsible. But are his ideas compatible with putting affordable food on all of our tables?

programme.desc: A look back at the impact of life in Scottish prisons and some special events where Gaelic communities put some old clothes stored in the loft to good use

programme.title: Mòd 2020
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald presents a celebration of previous Mòds, featuring many evocative performances from the winners of the Children's competitions

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Ceitidh receives a gift from Calum in the post, but it comes with a nasty surprise. Lucas carries on preparing the hall for the wedding

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: The lads take on a self-enforced challenge to cook vegetarian food, including cauliflower steak, Lebanese Fattouche salad, halloumi and courgette fries, and noodle miso soup

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: As Fizz gets ready to travel to another dimension populated entirely by other Fizzes, Magoo turns up and thinks that Weasel has a new kitten.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for billionaire Bruce Payne, aka the superhero, Ratman. When Ratman is kidnapped, Magoo decides to save the day.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tends to Fizz's toothache, but Magoo turns up, thinking he's busting into an animal testing lab.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Colonel Janus imposes a strict set of restrictions on the Thunderbirds after a series of mishaps nearly ends in disaster during a joint operation

programme.title: Dare Master at Home
programme.desc: Danny has to try to empty a paddling pool full of balls without using his hands.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Hank has to pretend to be his dad as he films his new action flick

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows a young boy and his two friends as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef is joined by guest Ben Miller, and chefs Mike Reid and Richard Corrigan, while there is also a masterclass in preparing lamb and a food adventure to London

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott re-creates his favourite takeaway dishes - Indian, Chinese and Italian. Guests Anita Rani and Jeremy Pang also help the chef prepare dishes

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of L'Isle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage from Newmarket, including the Dewhurst Stakes and the Cesarewitch. Plus, three races from York. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Oli Bell, Matt Chapman and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with former footballer-turned-TV-presenter Dion Dublin, Countdown's Rachel Riley and comedian Tim Vine all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Susan Calman, Dermot Murnaghan, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Omid Djalili pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The 14th series of the talent search comes to a close with the spectacular grand final. The remaining acts - including comedians Steve Royle and Jon Courtenay, dancing duo Aaron and Jasmine, and magician Magical Bones - go head to head to win the hearts and votes of the viewing public, with the winner landing a £250,000 prize and a slot at this year's Royal Variety Performance. Presented by Ant and Dec, with judges Amanda Holden, David Walliams, Alesha Dixon and Ashley Banjo

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and his co-host comedian Mawaan Rizwan welcome some of the UK's best new stand-ups to the comedy club, including the hilarious Aurie Styla, Fern Brady and Babatunde Aléshé. Plus, special guest Russell Kane will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Raging Bull (1980)
programme.desc: Biopic of boxer Jake LaMotta, who rose to fame as the world middleweight champion in the 1940s. His career was marred by an inability to deal with his anger, leaving his family bearing the brunt of his rage and paranoia. Directed by Martin Scorsese and starring an Oscar-winning Robert De Niro alongside Joe Pesci and Cathy Moriarty

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Anne Hegerty hosts a quiz show in which 16 families take part in a knock out tournament as they attempt to win the holiday of a lifetime and the title of Britain's Brightest Family. In the fourth episode, the Pettigrew family from Scotland and the Osholiki family from Dorset battle for a place in the quarter-finals

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dana and Grandma decide which one out of three dinosaurs would win in a fight, based on their size, strength, and various skills

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, hunting for Pinkbeard's treasure, mistakes the park for an island. Fizz and Weasel are there, selling ice cream that never falls on the ground.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo has decided to go mountain climbing but in reality, he is climbing up a skyscraper! He mixes Fizz up with a wounded bird and ruins his plan

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is peeling off old wallpaper on Linda's walls, but he is actually destroying Fizz' hypnotic posters.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A central air-traffic control facility suddenly goes offline, putting Kayo and the rest of International Rescue to the test

programme.desc: Dare Master Danny builds a toilet roll tower and tries to knock over as many skittles as he can using a ball in a pair of tights on his head

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Susan Calman, Dermot Murnaghan, Perri Shakes-Drayton and Omid Djalili pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Martin and Roman Kemp present the Sunday morning entertainment show, joined by writer-presenter Konnie Huq, Katherine Parkinson - soon to be seen in Channel 4's Taskmaster - and Rick Edwards, co-host of Sky's carpentry competition The Chop

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on her favourite countryside walk through the rolling plains of Cheshire, a trail with eight counties on show, two castles to explore and spectacular views across to Wales and Liverpool. A popular route for dog-walkers and sports, her trek proves to be full of surprises

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan is joined this week by two of his Hampshire neighbours, actress Sarah Parish and property expert Phil Spencer. He also meets online gardening sensation Huw Richards and Adrian Thomas of the RSPB, and carpenter Vikkie Lee is back with a new upcycling build. Plus, tips from Clodagh McKenna's garden at Highclere

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford find out about Welsh life through the ages on a visit to St Fagans National Museum of History in Cardiff. Plus, the man from Hampshire who says talking to the trees can improve a person's health

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Paul Heiney heads to the Dorset coast to visit one of the world's largest populations of swans. Ben Fogle travels to Northern Ireland in search of the elusive Irish hare and Liz Bonnin ventures out into the Atlantic to snorkel with basking sharks

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents coverage of the men's singles final in the concluding Grand Slam of the year at Roland-Garros in Paris. Rafael Nadal dominated this tournament once again last year, overcoming Dominic Thiem 6-3, 5-7, 6-1, 6-1 in the showpiece match to win this title for an incredible 12th time. With analysis from Jim Courier, Mark Petchey and Fabrice Santoro, and commentary by Nick Mullins

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, with highlights including crashing drones, flying dirtbikes and popping skulls

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as The Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker, Emmerdale actor Chris Bisson and reality star Joey Essex take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees the Phillips family from Bristol take on the Buckleys from Bury in Greater Manchester. The team who score the most points go through to Big Money, which sees two of them answering just five questions for the £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Walter spots an opportunity to turn a tragic situation to his advantage. As Singapore continues to face bombardment, Joan and Monty look to make their escape - but she refuses to leave empty handed - while Matthew and Vera desperately battle panicking crowds as they attempt to secure her safety

programme.title: UEFA Nations League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the third round of fixtures including England v Denmark at Wembley Stadium, Republic of Ireland v Wales at Aviva Stadium, Scotland v Slovakia at Hampden Park and Northern Ireland v Austria at Windsor Park. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Roy Keane and Ian Wright and commentary by Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Joe Speight, Seb Hutchinson, Joshua Adu-Donkor and Tom Gayle

programme.title: Prince William: A Planet for Us All
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge as he embarks on a campaign to champion global action on conservation and climate change. As he meets inspiring local heroes around Britain and the world, he sees an opportunity to become a bridge between the young people who are crying out for change and the leaders and policy-makers who can make it happen

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, featuring round four of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and round six of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents the penultimate round from Donington Park. With just 12 points separating the top four riders in the championship, there was all to play for in this event

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five football friends answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Mark, Abrar, Marilyn and Lucy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is shocked to discover Ellis has spent the night with Dawn, but she justifies her actions, insisting to Will she had to do the one thing Billy would never forgive her for. Charity is feeling paranoid about the state of her and Vanessa's relationship, while Victoria and Luke look over the land she has inherited from Annie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd puts pressure on Eileen to go ahead with his plan, while Scott tells Johnny of his plot to rip off Ray and wonders if he would like in on the action. David is horrified when he catches Shona about to take Max's medication instead of her own and realises with heavy heart that she's not safe to be left on her own

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: Ambulance driver Lee picks up a cat whose excess weight is causing it some serious health problems, while vet Charlotte deals with a husky with a sore paw in the mobile clinic. In Grimsby, Bev operates on a dog that has a worrying lump in its bladder that needs removing as soon as possible

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eileen tells a relieved Billy that it wasn't Todd's body in the mortuary and also confesses to Mary that Todd is alive and well and that she has been meeting up with him in secret. Johnny tells Scott to count him out, as his criminal days are behind him, and Ray reveals that he would like to buy the factory

programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian follows the journey of two beluga whales being released from captivity in an entertainment park in China and returned to the ocean. John's mission starts when he flies to Shanghai and visits the Beluga Theatre at Changfeng Park, where Little Grey and Little White have spent every day for the last 10 years performing for the crowds

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Martin Luther King by Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: To mark 50 years since the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King on April 4, 1968, Trevor McDonald travels to America's Deep South in the hope of getting closer to the man who meant so much to him and so many others. He uncovers new sides to the story, speaks to a former member of the Ku Klux Klan and interviews an expert on the horrors of lynching in 20th-century America. He also asks some of black role models, including Naomi Campbell, General Colin Powell and the Rev Al Sharpton, what Martin Luther King means to them

programme.title: Alison Hammond: Back to School
programme.desc: Alison Hammond travels across the country and back in time on an historical journey to discover some of the black figures who have been hidden from Britain's history, throwing light on their extraordinary stories and achievements

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of AEW, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five football friends answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Mark, Abrar, Marilyn and Lucy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Will is shocked to discover Ellis has spent the night with Dawn, but she justifies her actions, insisting to Will she had to do the one thing Billy would never forgive her for. Charity is feeling paranoid about the state of her and Vanessa's relationship, while Victoria and Luke look over the land she has inherited from Annie

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Todd puts pressure on Eileen to go ahead with his plan, while Scott tells Johnny of his plot to rip off Ray and wonders if he would like in on the action. David is horrified when he catches Shona about to take Max's medication instead of her own and realises with heavy heart that she's not safe to be left on her own

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: Ambulance driver Lee picks up a cat whose excess weight is causing it some serious health problems, while vet Charlotte deals with a husky with a sore paw in the mobile clinic. In Grimsby, Bev operates on a dog that has a worrying lump in its bladder that needs removing as soon as possible

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Eileen tells a relieved Billy that it wasn't Todd's body in the mortuary and also confesses to Mary that Todd is alive and well and that she has been meeting up with him in secret. Johnny tells Scott to count him out, as his criminal days are behind him, and Ray reveals that he would like to buy the factory

programme.title: John Bishop's Great Whale Rescue
programme.desc: Part one of two. The comedian follows the journey of two beluga whales being released from captivity in an entertainment park in China and returned to the ocean. John's mission starts when he flies to Shanghai and visits the Beluga Theatre at Changfeng Park, where Little Grey and Little White have spent every day for the last 10 years performing for the crowds

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Martin Luther King by Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: To mark 50 years since the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King on April 4, 1968, Trevor McDonald travels to America's Deep South in the hope of getting closer to the man who meant so much to him and so many others. He uncovers new sides to the story, speaks to a former member of the Ku Klux Klan and interviews an expert on the horrors of lynching in 20th-century America. He also asks some of black role models, including Naomi Campbell, General Colin Powell and the Rev Al Sharpton, what Martin Luther King means to them

programme.title: Alison Hammond: Back to School
programme.desc: Alison Hammond travels across the country and back in time on an historical journey to discover some of the black figures who have been hidden from Britain's history, throwing light on their extraordinary stories and achievements

Comedy, starring Amanda Barrie, Kenneth Williams, Sid James, Kenneth Connor, Jim Dale, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prominent resident is robbed and battered to death with her walking stick, and suspicion soon falls on the members of her reading club - which turns out to be a front for a lucrative investment portfolio. John Nettles and Daniel Casey star, with guest appearances by Anton Rodgers (Fresh Fields) and Angela Thorne (To the Manor Born)

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. When the detective suffers a personal loss, he's forced to navigate a complex murder case while grieving. A young Estonian woman has been found dead, with the evidence suggesting she was buried alive. Piecing together her movements, the team uncovers a dark world of prostitution and drugs, and investigates her connection to a single father, his autistic son, and a family who seem to have silenced the town

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of eminent lawyer Anaan Kamel is found washed up by an underground river, suspicion falls on Marcus Layton, a former employee of the victim's chambers who was recently fired by her husband Raheel. Text messages suggest an affair that had progressed to blackmail, but Banks and Morton struggle to find further information about Marcus, as his records only seem to date back five years. And when his corpse is found upstream in what looks to be a contract killing, the case becomes much more complicated

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part two of two. Joanna completes her Cuban journey by visiting the infamous town of Guantanamo. Giving the prison a wide berth, she discovers that the song Guantanamera was born in the town, and meets a talented young singer who is recording the track herself. She then heads across the Windward Passage to Haiti, currently the poorest country in the Western World. She visits the Citadel, a mighty fortress built to deter colonial powers, before taking in a Voodoo ceremony on the Iles a Vaches

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: When word reaches that a storm is headed for town, Kate refuses to toe the line, much to the grief of Councillor Bone. Koji discovers he has been given a jacket with an unfortunate history, and Medium may have lost Kate a lot of money on the horses. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Geoff now a marked man, he and daughter Evie are put under watch at a hotel while the search for Mullen is widened. However, when the suspect follows Evie's boyfriend to the location and sets off the fire alarm, he draws Geoff and Evie outside. Meanwhile, Banks believes loan shark Hargreaves is behind the hit on Marcus, and manages to extend his stay in the cell, despite having very little evidence against him. The conclusion of the two-part case, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of student Josh Tate is found dumped in a ravine, the investigation into his stabbing uncovers a very different man to the quiet character described by his flatmate Spencer. It emerges that Josh was infatuated with Spencer's girlfriend and had been manufacturing Ecstasy for club owner Liam Fallon. Meanwhile, DI Morton's ex-lover Martin Hexton has arrived, claiming to be working on a local case. When Fallon comes under suspicion as Josh's murderer the detectives break into his flat and find him badly beaten - and to complicate matters, it looks as if Hexton is responsible

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. Morton works hard to win back Banks's approval and discovers Hexton is the biological father of murdered student Josh. The team traces the former undercover police officer to Gerald and Elaine Foster's house, but it's too late - Hexton has kidnapped lap-dancer Melanie. When his victim collapses, however, he takes her to hospital, where ex-lover Helen corners him and has him arrested. What will he reveal about Josh and Melanie, and the attack on club owner Liam? Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.desc: When psychologist Reuben Beatty is murdered while moonlighting as a psychic, Lewis and Hathaway struggle to unravel the victim's baffling double life. programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Mollie Sugden as a hired-help who finds her long-lost son

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Grizzled copper Jack Frost teams up with an obnoxious, chain-smoking colleague to investigate a seemingly straightforward hit-and-run incident that proves to have devastating repercussions for the victim's family and their peaceful rural community. David Jason, Bruce Alexander and Philip Jackson star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Alf poses as a criminal as part of an undercover operation, but his covert actions only reveal startling information about Miller's ethical beliefs. David eyes up Peggy's room when she applies to be Lord Ashfordly's housekeeper - but both are left disappointed when the interview is a complete failure. Rural drama, starring William Simons, John Duttine, David Stockwell and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa and Albert's wedding day arrives. Steve gives Kim an engagement ring and is anxious for them to marry as soon as possible. Betty tells Zak that Lisa really wants him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary acts as peacemaker between Judy and Joyce. Maud is thrilled that Maureen and Bill are going away together for Christmas. Sean is set upon by two thugs outside the bookies

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is annoyed when Tony tells her he wants to finish with Maxine. Roy turns to Deirdre for help when Alec won't leave him alone. Liz is shaken when she hears Sean was beaten up

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bell-ringers of Midsomer Wellow are mysteriously murdered one by one - and it seems an ancient rhyme may hold the key to the killer's motive. As Barnaby and Troy embark on a baffling investigation in an attempt to determine the truth, Joyce starts digging into the past to discover the assassin's motives. John Nettles, Daniel Casey and Hugh Bonneville star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is asked to investigate the theft of a car belonging to a friend of Mullett, but misjudges the grizzled Superintendent's capacity for tact and diplomacy and it is not long before he is taken off the case. Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar return as detectives Cassie and Sunny, who find themselves thrown into another historic murder case with the discover of a body. This time it's the perfectly preserved remains of a middle-aged man found in a suitcase in the silt of a London river. As the detectives begin the complicated task of trying to identify the victim, four unconnected people go about their business - Brighton-based lawyer Colin Osborne, paediatric nurse Marion Kelsey, teacher Sara Mahmoud and respected police officer Tessa Nixon - unaware they are about to be dragged into the investigation. Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar star, with Mark Bonnar, Lorraine Ashbourne, Charlie Condou and Wendy Craig

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam
